prestazioni: Engine swap to a Mercedes-Benz OM662 2.9L turbocharged diesel (D290) inline-5 with forged Mahle pistons (83mm), Carrillo H-beam connecting rods, and a Garrett GT2260V turbocharger (0.82 A/R turbine housing) for 280hp and 450Nm torque, paired with a reinforced Getrag 6-speed manual transmission (from the SsangYong Rexton) and a Sachs Performance twin-disc clutch (380mm diameter).

Full custom 3-inch stainless steel exhaust system by Milltek Sport, featuring a 4-2-1 merge collector, 200-cell catalytic converter, and a titanium-wrapped Borla Pro XS muffler for optimized backpressure and acoustic tuning, specifically designed for the OM662’s torque curve.

Custom ECU remapping by Viezu Technologies (K-Suite) with a standalone Motec M150 ECU for precise fueling and timing control, including a 2-bar MAP sensor, AEM X-Series wideband O2 sensor, and a custom intercooler upgrade using a PWR 450x220x76mm core with silicone couplers from Vibrant Performance, reducing intake temps by 25°C under full load.
estetica: Full Rocket Bunny Pandem-style widebody kit (Korando C-specific, not a generic SUV kit) with carbon fiber fender flares, front splitter, and rear diffuser, paired with a custom 3M Matte Military Green wrap (2080 series) and blacked-out trim using Lamin-X protective film.

Ohlins Road & Track DFV coilovers (custom valved for the Korando C’s 1,850kg weight) with 80mm extended top mounts, adjustable camber plates, and helper springs for a 50mm drop, paired with Whiteline adjustable sway bars (24mm front, 22mm rear) and poly bushings from SuperPro (part #SPF1039K).

Custom 19x9.5 ET25 Enkei RS05RR wheels in Hyper Silver with 245/35 R19 Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires, paired with Brembo GT 6-piston front calipers (355mm discs) and 4-piston rear calipers (330mm discs), using custom stainless steel braided lines from Goodridge and Ferodo DS2500 brake pads.
